chunks.text:
About Son Bui
## Who is Son Bui

Son Bui (full name Bui Thanh Son, Vietnamese: Bùi Thanh Sơn — he goes by "Son") is a Software Engineer in Test (SDET) based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, and open to remote work worldwide. Born in 2003, he is originally from Bao Loc City in Lam Dong Province, where he grew up in a coffee-farming family. His focus is intelligent test platforms, framework architecture, and testing infrastructure that make software quality scalable across an engineering organization.

His one-line positioning: **"I build AI-powered automation platforms — not just automated tests."**

## What makes his work distinctive

Son treats automation as software engineering rather than script writing — software quality is an engineering problem, and testing should be designed like software. He believes good automation should be scalable, maintainable, observable, reusable, easy to extend, and platform-oriented; he values engineering systems over isolated scripts, and architecture over automation volume. He measures success not by the number of tests written, but by how much repetitive engineering effort his platforms eliminate for the whole team. His signature project is an AI-powered, multi-agent test-automation platform that takes a Jira ticket all the way to a production-ready pull request.

## Areas of strength

He works across the testing lifecycle and the stack: reusable Playwright + TypeScript frameworks, cross-platform desktop testing with Squish, CI/CD and automation infrastructure, and AI agent systems built on the Claude Agent SDK and the Model Context Protocol (MCP). He also has full-stack engineering experience (Java, Spring Boot, MySQL, Angular/React) that informs how he designs testable systems.

## Current role

Son **currently works at OPSWAT Vietnam** (enterprise cybersecurity) as an **Associate Software Engineer in Test (SDET), from November 2024 to the present**. Before that he worked as a contract Front-end Developer at Rovi Holdings Investment JSC (Sep–Oct 2024), and earlier gained professional AI experience building NLP models with a small team.

## Availability

Son is open to Senior SDET and Test Platform engineering roles, remote-friendly. He can be reached at sonbuithanh306@gmail.com, on GitHub at github.com/sontbui, and on LinkedIn.

## Tóm tắt tiếng Việt (Vietnamese summary)

Sơn (Bùi Thanh Sơn) sinh năm 2003, quê ở Bảo Lộc, Lâm Đồng, lớn lên trong gia đình nông dân trồng cà phê, hiện sống tại TP. Hồ Chí Minh. **Sơn đang làm việc tại OPSWAT Việt Nam** (an ninh mạng doanh nghiệp) với vai trò **Associate Software Engineer in Test (SDET) từ tháng 11/2024 đến nay**. Tốt nghiệp Cử nhân Công nghệ Thông tin tại Đại học Mở TP.HCM năm 2025. **Giải Ba Nghiên cứu Khoa học khoa CNTT Đại học Mở năm 2024** với đề tài app đặt đồ ăn trên mobile (iOS & Android) có tính năng nhận diện món ăn bằng CNN YOLOv8 — chụp tô phở là app nhận ra món và gợi ý quán ngon gần nhất. Trước đây từng đi làm AI cho một nhóm nhỏ, build model phục vụ các bài toán xử lý ngôn ngữ tự nhiên (NLP). Các dự án nổi bật: nền tảng AI tự động hoá kiểm thử đa agent (Jira ticket → Pull Request), framework Playwright doanh nghiệp, hạ tầng kiểm thử đa nền tảng, và Beacon — desktop app "hệ điều hành AI cho kỹ sư". Mục tiêu: Senior SDET, xa hơn là thiết kế các nền tảng kỹ thuật ứng dụng AI.

chunks.text:
Engineering Achievements, Awards & Impact
Awards & recognition
- **3rd Prize — Scientific Research Competition, Faculty of Information Technology, Ho Chi Minh City Open University (2024).** Son's research project was a **food-ordering mobile app** (supporting both **iOS and Android**) whose standout feature was **AI food recognition built on a CNN using YOLOv8**: photograph a dish — say, a bowl of phở — and the app identifies it by name and suggests the best-rated nearby restaurants serving it. The project combined computer vision, mobile development, and location-based recommendations into one working product, and reflects Son's habit of building end-to-end systems rather than isolated demos.

Engineering Achievements, Awards & Impact
Professional AI / NLP experience
Beyond test automation, Son has hands-on professional AI experience: he worked with a small team **building machine-learning models for Natural Language Processing tasks**. This background in training and applying models — both NLP and computer vision (YOLOv8) — is part of why he treats AI as an engineering component he understands from the inside, not a black box he merely calls.

Education, Languages & Background
Roots & upbringing
Son was born in **2003** in **Bao Loc City, Lam Dong Province, Vietnam**, and now lives in **Ho Chi Minh City**. He grew up in a **farming family — his parents grow coffee**. That environment taught him persistence, discipline, responsibility, humility, and the habit of working hard from an early age, and those values still shape how he approaches engineering today: steady, honest work over shortcuts.

Personality
Son is naturally open and easy to talk to. In technical discussions he is straightforward and honest — he values truth over ego, is comfortable admitting mistakes, and treats mistakes as opportunities to improve. He enjoys discussing architecture, engineering decisions, automation, backend systems, and AI, and prefers constructive technical debate over simply agreeing.

Education
Son holds a **Bachelor's degree in Computer Science** from **Ho Chi Minh City Open University (Đại học Mở)**, graduated in **2025**.

chunks.text:
Education, Languages & Background
How Son learns
One of Son's greatest strengths is learning new technologies quickly. When exploring something new, he rarely spends weeks studying before building — instead he rapidly creates **proofs of concept**, because building an MVP is the fastest way to validate an idea. Once the concept is validated, he iterates on the design and improves it. This POC-first habit is how he adopted the Claude Agent SDK, MCP, and n8n quickly for his AI platform, and Electron for Beacon.

chunks.text:
Education, Languages & Background
Career journey — the OPSWAT chapter
Son's professional growth has been shaped primarily by **OPSWAT**. Working alongside experienced senior engineers there, he didn't just accumulate experience — he learned **how experienced engineers think**: how they review systems, design maintainable software, and make long-term technical decisions. Much of his engineering discipline, architecture thinking, testing practice, and platform-design philosophy was developed and refined at OPSWAT, and he considers it the environment that most accelerated his growth.

chunks.text:
Engineering Principles & Philosophy
## Core philosophy

"Automation is software engineering. I measure success not by the number of tests I write, but by how much engineering effort my platforms eliminate." Every automation solution should reduce engineering effort in the long term — not simply make today's test pass.

## Principles Son follows

- **Build maintainable systems over quick fixes.** Design for the teammate who extends it in six months, not the demo today.
- **Design reusable frameworks, not duplicated solutions.** Solve a class of problems once and let the platform scale it.
- **Automate workflows, not just test cases.** The biggest wins come from removing whole steps, not scripting one.
- **Developer experience is a first-class concern.** Fast feedback and clear reporting are what make automation actually get used.
- **Use AI to augment engineering, not replace thinking.** AI handles the repetitive; engineers keep ownership of judgment.

## How Son approaches software quality

He treats a test framework as a product for engineers: clear fixtures, fast feedback, and readable reports drive real adoption. He favours fixture-driven architecture with shared utilities over per-suite convenience — more upfront design, but reuse and maintainability that compound across every product. Reliability is engineered explicitly through flaky-test detection, retry, quarantine, and trend analysis.

## Why Software Engineer in Test

Son is drawn to the leverage of the role: instead of testing one feature, he builds the platforms and frameworks that let an entire organization ship quality faster. He enjoys problems where the win is removing repetitive engineering — turning a manual, linear-effort workflow into a scalable system.

meta.title: Engineering Principles & Philosophy
meta.summary: The principles that guide how Son Bui approaches software quality and automation — treating automation as software engineering that reduces long-term effort.

chunks.text:
Work Experience
## Associate Software Engineer in Test (SDET) — OPSWAT Vietnam

**Industry:** Enterprise Cybersecurity · **Period:** Nov 2024 — Present

At OPSWAT, Son designed and built the team's reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework, moving the team from a fully manual process to scalable hybrid automation. He architected an AI-powered automation proof-of-concept (Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n) that automates the path from a Jira ticket to a pull request.

He established automation architecture and engineering standards and reviewed automation design across the team. His work grew automation coverage from 0 to roughly 80% with around 700 cross-platform tests, cut regression execution time by about 70%, and reduced flaky tests from roughly 150 to fewer than 20.

**Technologies:** Playwright, TypeScript, Squish, Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n, CI/CD.

## Front-end Developer (Contract) — Rovi Holdings Investment JSC

**Industry:** Technology & Investment · **Period:** Sep 2024 — Oct 2024

Son built responsive, type-safe React components that improved maintainability and reduced duplication. He integrated backend APIs and contributed to requirement analysis, testing, and debugging, delivering the contract scope on a short timeline without sacrificing code quality.

**Technologies:** React, TypeScript, Material UI.

## AI / NLP Engineer — small team engagement

Earlier in his journey, Son worked professionally with a small team **building machine-learning models for Natural Language Processing tasks**. Combined with the computer-vision work in his prize-winning research project (YOLOv8 food recognition), this hands-on model-building experience is why he treats AI as an engineering component he understands from the inside — training, evaluating, and shipping models, not just calling APIs.

chunks.text:
Frequently Asked Questions (EN & Tiếng Việt)
What is Son's AI experience? / Kinh nghiệm AI của Sơn?
**EN:** Three layers: (1) **model building** — professional NLP model work with a small team, plus YOLOv8 computer vision in his prize-winning app; (2) **agentic AI platforms** — the multi-agent test-automation platform (Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n) that takes a Jira ticket to a pull request in ~20 minutes; (3) **AI products** — Beacon, a desktop "AI operating system for engineers" with automatic AI code review. He understands AI from training models to orchestrating agents to shipping AI products.

**VI:** Ba lớp: (1) **build model** — NLP chuyên nghiệp + computer vision YOLOv8; (2) **nền tảng AI đa agent** — từ Jira ticket ra Pull Request trong ~20 phút (Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n); (3) **sản phẩm AI** — Beacon, desktop app với AI review code tự động. Sơn hiểu AI từ huấn luyện model đến điều phối agent đến sản phẩm hoàn chỉnh.
headingPath: What is Son's AI experience? / Kinh nghiệm AI của Sơn?

Frequently Asked Questions (EN & Tiếng Việt)
What is Son's biggest achievement? / Thành tựu lớn nhất của Sơn?
**EN:** Architecting the AI-powered automation platform that removes repetitive engineering between a requirement and its verification — alongside taking OPSWAT's targeted product from 0 to ~80% automation coverage (~700 cross-platform tests, regression time −70%, flaky tests 150 → <20), shipping Beacon (278+ internal builds), and the 2024 research prize.

**VI:** Kiến trúc nền tảng tự động hoá AI (ticket → PR), đưa coverage từ 0 lên ~80% với ~700 test đa nền tảng tại OPSWAT (regression −70%, flaky 150 → <20), ship Beacon (278+ bản build nội bộ), và Giải Ba NCKH 2024.

Frequently Asked Questions (EN & Tiếng Việt)
Why did Son choose SDET? / Tại sao Sơn chọn làm SDET?
**EN:** Because software quality is an engineering problem, and SDET sits exactly where engineering, architecture, and product quality meet. Son saw that the biggest wins come from removing whole classes of repetitive work — building frameworks and platforms — rather than writing individual test cases. SDET lets him combine backend engineering, infrastructure, and now AI into systems that scale quality across a whole organization.

**VI:** Vì chất lượng phần mềm là một bài toán kỹ thuật, và SDET là điểm giao của engineering, kiến trúc và chất lượng sản phẩm. Sơn tin thắng lợi lớn nhất đến từ việc loại bỏ cả một lớp công việc lặp lại — xây framework, xây platform — chứ không phải viết từng test case.
headingPath: Why did Son choose SDET? / Tại sao Sơn chọn làm SDET?

chunks.text:
Frequently Asked Questions (EN & Tiếng Việt)
What are Son's weaknesses? / Điểm yếu của Sơn?
**EN:** Son is candid about this — his Beacon case study openly documents that he shipped his own product without an automated test suite (solo-project momentum) and names it as the exact trap he helps teams avoid at work. He treats weaknesses as engineering debts: name them, write them down, sequence the fix. For a fuller picture, this is a great question to ask him directly in an interview.

**VI:** Sơn thẳng thắn về điều này — case study Beacon công khai việc anh ship sản phẩm cá nhân mà chưa có test suite tự động, và gọi đó đúng là cái bẫy anh giúp team tránh trong công việc. Cách của Sơn: gọi tên điểm yếu, ghi lại, và lên lộ trình sửa. Muốn nghe đầy đủ hơn, đây là câu rất đáng hỏi trực tiếp khi phỏng vấn.
headingPath: What are Son's weaknesses? / Điểm yếu của Sơn?

chunks.text:
Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
The story arc
Son was born in **2003** in **Bao Loc, Lam Dong** — Vietnam's coffee highlands. His parents are coffee farmers, and he grew up inside the rhythm of that work: seasons you can't rush, harvests that reward patience, and problems you fix with your own hands because no one else will. He carried three things from that farm into engineering: **persistence** (coffee doesn't grow faster because you're frustrated), **discipline** (the work happens every day, not when inspired), and **humility** (nature doesn't care about your ego — and neither do production systems).

From Bao Loc he went to Ho Chi Minh City to study Computer Science at Open University (graduated **2025**). In **2024** — while still studying — he won **3rd prize in the faculty's scientific research competition** with a mobile food-ordering app whose YOLOv8-based food recognition could name a dish from a photo. He also worked professionally with a small team building **NLP models**, did a front-end contract at **Rovi Holdings**, and in **November 2024** joined **OPSWAT Vietnam** as an Associate SDET — the role that shaped his engineering identity.

meta.title: Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
meta.summary: The fuller personal picture of Son Bui — from a coffee farm in Bao Loc to building AI platforms; his values, personality in practice, learning style, communication style, and goals. English and Vietnamese.

chunks.text:
Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
Personality, in practice
Son is open and easy to talk to — the kind of teammate you can raise a half-formed idea with. But in technical discussions he switches to a different gear: **direct, honest, truth over ego**. He'll tell you when he thinks a design is wrong, and he expects the same back. He is genuinely comfortable saying "I was wrong" — he treats mistakes as compressed lessons, not embarrassments. The sync bug in Beacon that wiped his own reminders became a design principle ("never let sync mean replace") and then a public case-study section. That's his pattern: mistake → principle → documentation.

He prefers **constructive debate over agreement**. A meeting where everyone nods worries him more than one with pushback.

meta.title: Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
meta.summary: The fuller personal picture of Son Bui — from a coffee farm in Bao Loc to building AI platforms; his values, personality in practice, learning style, communication style, and goals. English and Vietnamese.

chunks.text:
Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
How he learns (and why it's fast)
Son's learning loop is **POC-first**: when something new appears, he doesn't study for weeks — he builds the smallest thing that could work, lets reality correct his misunderstandings, then iterates the design. Evidence across his work: he picked up the Claude Agent SDK, MCP, and n8n fast enough to ship an AI automation POC; learned Electron by shipping Beacon (278+ builds); learned YOLOv8 by shipping a prize-winning app. He'll tell you the first version is always partly wrong — the point is finding out *which part* quickly.
headingPath: How he learns (and why it's fast)

meta.title: Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works

chunks.text:
Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
How he works
- **Platforms over scripts:** if a problem will recur, he builds the system that solves the class of problems. His framework work at OPSWAT and both AI platforms follow this.
- **Honest engineering ledgers:** he names technical debt in writing (Beacon's README ships its own threat model). He believes documented weakness is a strength signal, not a weakness.
- **Human judgment stays human:** every AI system he builds has explicit human gates — approval before publishing test plans, review before merging PRs, opt-in before AI posts anything.
- **Developer experience matters:** he measures his platforms by whether the team actually adopts them, not by feature count.

chunks.text:
Son as a Person — Story, Values & How He Works
What drives him
The through-line of everything Son builds is **removing repetitive engineering work so humans can do judgment work**. The coffee-farm kid who watched his parents do unavoidably manual labor now builds systems so engineers don't have to. Near-term goal: **Senior SDET**. Long-term: designing **AI-powered engineering platforms** that combine AI, backend engineering, distributed systems, and testing — the intersection he's been circling from three directions (NLP models → test automation → AI products).

Outside work: **badminton** and **football**, played regularly and competitively enough to care about the score.

Tóm tắt tiếng Việt


Sơn sinh năm **2003** ở **Bảo Lộc, Lâm Đồng** — thủ phủ cà phê. Ba mẹ làm nông, trồng cà phê; tuổi thơ đó cho Sơn ba thứ mang thẳng vào nghề kỹ sư: **kiên trì** (cà phê không chín nhanh hơn vì mình sốt ruột), **kỷ luật** (việc phải làm mỗi ngày, không phải khi có hứng), và **khiêm tốn** (hệ thống production không quan tâm cái tôi của mình). Học CNTT ĐH Mở TP.HCM (tốt nghiệp 2025), **Giải Ba NCKH 2024** với app nhận diện món ăn YOLOv8, từng làm NLP cho một nhóm nhỏ, contract front-end ở Rovi Holdings, và từ **11/2024 là SDET tại OPSWAT Việt Nam**.

Cách học: **làm POC trước** — build bản nhỏ nhất chạy được để thực tế sửa hiểu lầm của mình, rồi mới tối ưu thiết kế; nhờ vậy học Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n, Electron, YOLOv8 đều rất nhanh. chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
# The AI Test Automation Pipeline — how it actually works

This is the production implementation behind Son's flagship "Jira ticket → pull request" platform. An interactive replay of it — real node names, states, endpoints, and log formats — runs at **/work/ai-pipeline** (the Pipeline Console demo). Identifiers below are sanitized; the architecture is exact.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
Two-phase architecture


The platform is **two separate n8n workflows** with a human approval gate between them:

**Phase I — Test Case Writer.** A scheduled/webhook-triggered workflow polls Jira for tickets moved to the "AI Analyzing" status, loops over them, filters by project, checks pipeline metadata (so a ticket is never processed twice), and calls a **ticket-writer agent** that researches the ticket (Jira + Confluence + codebase), drafts a structured test plan, and writes it to a review file. The pipeline then **waits for a human**: only when a QA lead marks the review file `Status: Approved` does a **jira-publisher agent** push the finalized test cases to the Jira ticket and comment. Machine proposes, human approves — by design.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
Two-phase architecture
**Phase II — Generate Test Script.** Picks up approved tickets. Steps: verify status and untouched metadata → **write a handoff file** (`.claude/AI-debug/handoff/<TICKET>.md` — a durable contract carrying the normalized requirement, approved test cases, agent mode, and reporter) → **generate-test-script agent** writes the Playwright spec → the spec is **executed immediately** → a gate branches on the result:
- **PASS** → post result comment to Jira → **git-and-pr agent** creates branch, commit, and pull request → Jira transitions to "In Review" with the PR link.
- **FAIL** → **run-and-debug-test agent** self-corrects (it may only fix AUTOMATION bugs, max 3 attempts) → re-run → **analyze-test-results agent** classifies the failure → if resolved, continue to delivery; if it's a PRODUCT bug, the pipeline halts and hands to a human.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
The agent host: qa-ai-service
Agents don't run inside n8n. n8n code-nodes call a small local Node.js service (**qa-ai-service**, port 3939) that hosts everything AI:

- `POST /v1/invoke` — runs a named agent (`generate-test-script`, `run-and-debug-test`, `git-and-pr`, `analyze-test-results`, ticket-writer, jira-publisher). Agent definitions are markdown files (`agents/<name>.md`) with skills and assets resolved into the prompt.
- `POST /v1/run-test` — executes a Playwright spec, returns pass/fail + duration + artifacts.
- Three AI modes: `copilot`, `claude-api` (direct Anthropic API, default model **claude-sonnet-4-5**), and `claude-cli` (Claude Code CLI — enables repo-aware runs where the agent can read the actual codebase).
- Real timeouts: agent invocations 900s; test execution up to 31 minutes; analyze 360s.
- Per-run metadata is posted back (states: `inprogress → passed | failed | error | completed`), including retryCount and PR URL extraction.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
Why these engineering decisions
- **n8n for orchestration, not code:** the workflow is visible, auditable, and editable by non-authors; every run has an execution ID and per-node I/O history. Orchestration logic and AI logic are cleanly separated.
- **Handoff files as contracts:** phases communicate through durable markdown files, not in-memory state — any phase can be re-run independently, and humans can read/edit the contract. This is the same "explicit contract at the boundary" principle as Beacon's IPC layer.
- **A local agent host instead of calling Claude from n8n:** one place owns prompt assembly, agent definitions, model selection, timeouts, and logging; n8n nodes stay thin HTTP callers.
- **Failure classification before retry:** the debug agent is only allowed to fix AUTOMATION defects (selector drift, timing). If analysis says PRODUCT bug, the pipeline stops — an AI that "fixes" tests to pass over real bugs would be worse than useless.
- **Human gates at both ends:** a person approves the test plan before code is generated, and a person reviews the PR before merge. The AI owns the repetitive middle, never the judgment.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
The live demo (/work/ai-pipeline)
The Pipeline Console replays this exact workflow as an orchestration UI: snake-layout phase rows (Intake → Generate & Execute → Self-Correction → Delivery), live statuses, streaming logs in the real log format, a per-node inspector (endpoint, model, tokens, cost, I/O payloads, artifacts — including the generated Playwright spec, the self-correction diff, and the PR summary), pause/resume/restart and speed controls. It alternates a happy-path run with a self-correction run. Durations are compressed; everything else mirrors production.

Measured results
~20 minutes from ticket to PR for a typical task (~10 test cases) in POC runs; the pipeline runs end-to-end with no manual step between requirement analysis and the pull request.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
Tóm tắt tiếng Việt
Pipeline AI của Sơn gồm **2 workflow n8n**: Phase I (Test Case Writer) nghiên cứu ticket Jira, soạn test plan, **chờ người duyệt** rồi mới đăng lên Jira; Phase II (Generate Test Script) viết handoff file, agent sinh Playwright spec, chạy ngay, nếu fail thì agent tự sửa (chỉ được sửa lỗi AUTOMATION, tối đa 3 lần), agent phân loại lỗi (AUTOMATION/PRODUCT), pass thì tự tạo branch + commit + Pull Request và chuyển ticket sang "In Review". Các agent chạy qua service nội bộ **qa-ai-service** (cổng 3939, model claude-sonnet-4-5, hỗ trợ Claude Code CLI để agent đọc được codebase). Demo tương tác tại **/work/ai-pipeline** tái hiện đúng workflow production (tên agent, endpoint, log format thật — chỉ nén thời gian). Kết quả đo được: ~20 phút từ ticket ra PR.

meta.title: AI Test Automation Pipeline — Production Architecture
meta.summary: Deep technical documentation of Son's production AI pipeline — two n8n workflows, a local agent-host service, six Claude agents, human approval gates, self-correction, and PR delivery. The live demo is at /work/ai-pipeline.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
## Overview

The AI-Powered Test Automation Platform is Son's flagship project (a proof of concept, actively expanding). It is a multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — requirement analysis, test generation, execution, failure triage, and delivery, orchestrated end to end.

## The problem

Test automation is usually treated as a manual engineering workflow: read the ticket, understand requirements, write the test, run it, debug the failure, open the PR. Every step is repetitive, and every engineer does it slightly differently — so effort scales linearly with the work.

## The insight

Treat AI not as a coding assistant but as an engineering component: specialized agents that each own a stage of the lifecycle and collaborate through a shared orchestration layer, with context supplied by the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The goal isn't more tests; it's removing the repetitive engineering between a requirement and its verification.

## Agent orchestration architecture

Context enters through **Jira MCP** and **Confluence MCP** connectors into an **Orchestrator** built on the Claude Agent SDK, n8n, and YAML workflows. The orchestrator coordinates six specialized agents and emits artifacts: Playwright tests, a Git branch and pull request, and a Jira comment. A self-correction loop feeds fixes from the Failure Analyst back to the Test Generator.

The six agents:

- **Requirement Analyst** — normalizes the ticket into testable requirements.
- **Knowledge Retriever** — pulls project context from Jira & Confluence.
- **Test Generator** — writes Playwright (TypeScript) automation.
- **Executor** — runs the suite and captures results.
- **Failure Analyst** — separates automation issues from real defects.
- **PR Author** — creates the branch, commits, PR, and Jira comment.

## Execution sequence (Ticket → PR)

1. **Ticket received** (Jira MCP) — requirements normalized into a testable spec.
2. **Context retrieved** — relevant project knowledge pulled from Jira & Confluence to ground generation.
3. **Tests generated** — Playwright TypeScript tests authored from requirements and context.
4. **Suite executed** — automation runs; results, traces, and failures collected.
5. **Failures triaged** — each failure classified as automation issue vs. genuine product defect.
6. **Self-correction loop** — flaky or fixable automation is corrected and re-run automatically.
7. **Delivered** — branch, commits, and PR created and the Jira ticket updated, ready for human review.

## Engineering decisions & trade-offs

- **AI as an engineering component, not an assistant.** Each agent owns one lifecycle stage with a clear contract, so the system is composable and debuggable — not a single opaque prompt.
- **MCP for context, not scraping.** MCP gives agents structured access to Jira & Confluence, keeping context accurate and integration maintainable.
- **Human-in-the-loop at the PR.** The platform stops at a pull request; delivery stays reviewable. AI does the repetitive work, engineers own the merge.
- **Orchestration via n8n + YAML.** Declarative workflows make the pipeline transparent and easy to extend without rewriting agent logic.

## Results (POC)

- ~20 minutes from ticket to PR for a typical task (~10 test cases).
- 6 specialized agents across the lifecycle.
- End-to-end with no manual step in between.

## What's next

Broaden coverage beyond Playwright to desktop/API generation; add confidence scoring so the platform knows when to defer to a human; and richer failure analytics feeding suite-health trends.

## Stack

Claude Agent SDK, Model Context Protocol, Jira MCP, Confluence MCP, Playwright, TypeScript, n8n, YAML, Git automation.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
## Overview

A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved the team at OPSWAT from a fully manual process to scalable hybrid automation. This is an enterprise project; it is presented through original architecture, without proprietary detail.

## The problem

Manual regression didn't scale and produced inconsistent coverage across enterprise products spanning multiple platforms.

## Key trade-off

Son chose a fixture-driven architecture with shared utilities over per-suite convenience — more upfront design, but reuse and maintainability that compound across every product.

## Lesson

A framework is a product for engineers. Developer experience — clear fixtures, fast feedback, readable reports — is what drives real adoption.

## Layered architecture

- **Test Specs** (UI · API · Backend) — readable, intent-focused test cases.
- **Fixtures** (lifecycle) — setup/teardown, auth, shared state.
- **Page & Component Objects** (abstraction) — encapsulated selectors and interactions.
- **Core Utilities** (shared) — helpers, data builders, API clients.
- **Config & Reporting** (platform) — environments, parallelism, CI integration.

## Parallel execution

One codebase fans out into N shards and merges into a single report, keeping feedback fast as the suite grows. This is the project that demonstrates Son's architecture thinking most directly: designing for reuse, composition, and developer experience rather than one-off scripts.

meta.title: Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
meta.summary: A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved an enterprise team from manual testing to scalable hybrid automation.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
# Beacon — An AI Operating System for Software Engineers

Full case study: /work/beacon (interactive architecture explorer, live demo, engineering timeline, metrics, downloads)

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
What it is
Beacon is a cross-platform Electron desktop application (macOS DMG & Windows NSIS installers) that unifies an engineer's day into one system: multi-repo GitHub review tracking with automatic AI code review, multi-project Jira sprint management, and an adaptive day planner that learns the user's real velocity. Claude is integrated as a governed engineering component — every AI run shows exact token cost, nothing posts without permission, and all AI features degrade gracefully when the Claude CLI isn't installed.

Son built Beacon solo — product, architecture, and delivery. It is an internal tool in daily use, with 278+ versioned internal builds shipped.

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Key features

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Key features
- **AI Daily Brief**: the landing dashboard — a deterministic base (exact numbers, computed instantly, never blocks on AI) enriched by a Claude narrative; cache-first, pre-generated in both UI languages (English/Vietnamese).
- **Auto AI review**: new PRs requesting the user's review are queued and reviewed by Claude one run at a time, with the team's Knowledge Hub as context; findings are saved with history and re-reviewed on new commits (60s settle window).
- **Adaptive Planner**: a pure-algorithm solver places work around meetings and lunch. Block state ladder: Reserved → Accepted → Placed → Locked; changes to accepted blocks go through a diff the user approves. The engine measures real beliefs (minutes per story point, review time, golden hours). AI Plan classifies tickets by role (Dev full effort, QA ~50%) and splits big tickets across days.
- **Sprint (Jira)**: multi-project sprint health, forecast (points remaining × measured velocity vs hours left), AI-drafted standups from real activity, retro boards.
- **Knowledge Hub**: per-repo team knowledge stored centrally; compiles to local-only Claude skills; "Learn from reviews" mines ~100 recent team review comments into proposed rules.
- Everything is wired together: a PR merging auto-completes its plan block; a Jira ticket hitting Done closes its schedule entry; notes become reminders and plan items.

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Architecture
Electron's three-process model used as a security architecture:

- **Renderer**: 12 product modules in dependency-free vanilla JavaScript (~13.9K lines) — no framework, no build step.
- **Preload bridge**: contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false; exactly 60 whitelisted invoke-style IPC channels (verb:object naming) — the whole UI capability surface in one auditable file.
- **Main process**: 13 feature stores (~1.4K lines) owning every privileged operation — Claude CLI engine, safe-by-construction git layer (fixed argv, strict branch regex, patches via stdin, force-push doesn't exist), Jira client, differential sync stores, OS-keychain secrets.
- **Data**: MongoDB as source of truth (5 collections), offline-first with disk cache, newer-side-wins conflict resolution, differential sync (bulk upserts keyed by owner+id — never delete-all/insert-all).
- Only 2 runtime dependencies: dotenv and mongodb.

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Engineering judgement highlights
- Chose Claude CLI over the API: reuses the engineer's own login (zero key management) and can read the actual codebase during review when run with the repo as cwd.
- Documented honest threat model in the README: the shipped MongoDB credential is AES-256-GCM encrypted but explicitly called obfuscation; three ranked remediation options with effort estimates; backend API + JWT is the top roadmap item.
- Known, named gap: no automated test suite (only a node --check syntax gate). Son discusses this openly — the sync-wipe bug that real usage found is exactly what a store-layer test would have caught, and the plan is to use his AI test-automation platform to generate Beacon's regression suite.
- Vanilla JS renderer was a deliberate zero-build-step bet that kept iteration fast to ~10K lines; past that Son says ES modules and a typed IPC contract would be the refactor.

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
How it connects to Son's other work
Beacon applies the same philosophy as his AI-Powered Test Automation Platform — AI as a governed engineering component, not an assistant — but as a product with users, accounts, onboarding, i18n, themes, and installers rather than a pipeline. The story across projects: tests → framework that writes tests → infrastructure that runs them → platform where AI generates them → Beacon, the thesis applied to an engineer's entire day. Platforms and systems, not just applications.

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Six engineering highlights (the code worth reading)
1. **A git layer that can't be abused** (`git-store.js`) — fixed argument vectors, no shell, branch names regex-validated, patches via stdin; force-push has no code path. Safe by construction, not by validation.
2. **Claude CLI as a cross-platform engine** (`claude-cli.js`) — binary resolution across PATH + install dirs; Windows .cmd shims run through a shell but user content only ever travels via stdin; token usage rides a side-channel so callers keep plain-text responses; timeouts scale with context (300s with repo access, 120s without).
3. **Sync that can't wipe your other machine** (`reminders-store.js`) — differential sync (bulk upserts keyed by owner+id, targeted deletes only) after a real two-machine data-loss bug; claim-once legacy migrations with atomic renames.
4. **A 60-channel security boundary you can read in one sitting** (`preload.js`) — contextIsolation on, nodeIntegration off, every UI capability explicitly enumerated; embedded GitHub/Jira webviews in isolated session partitions.
5. **Offline-first without a sync framework** (`mongo.js`) — lazy singleton client, retry-after backoff, MONGO_OFFLINE fast-fail, disk-first atomic writes, newer-side-wins on reconnect. CRDTs would have been résumé-driven development.
6. **Secrets engineering with an honest threat model** (`env-crypt.js` + README) — OS-keychain tokens, scrypt passwords, AES-256-GCM for the shipped Mongo URI — documented as obfuscation, with three ranked remediation options and effort estimates.
headingPath: Six engineering highlights (the code worth reading)

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Engineering journey (phases)
Problem discovery (tab-archaeology mornings) → architecture design (Electron three-process as a security architecture; vanilla JS renderer as a zero-build-step bet) → MVP (a PR tracker — package.json still says `pr-tracker`) → AI integration (review pipeline, Daily Brief, AI Plan — CLI over API for zero key management + repo-aware review) → multi-account & sync hardening (the bugs only real usage finds) → productization (onboarding, EN/VI i18n, admin accounts, encrypted packaging, 278+ builds) → honest ledger (two named debts: client-held DB credential and no automated tests) → roadmap (backend API + JWT first; then a test suite generated by his own AI platform — there's poetry in that).

meta.title: Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
meta.summary: A cross-platform Electron desktop platform unifying multi-repo GitHub reviews, Jira sprints, and an adaptive AI planner — Son Bui's featured engineering case study, built solo.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
What Son would change today
TypeScript at the IPC boundary from day one (the 60-channel contract is disciplined but stringly-typed), and ES modules once the renderer passed ~10K lines — the zero-build-step bet paid off early and started costing later.

chunks.text:
Beacon — AI Operating System for Software Engineers
Tóm tắt tiếng Việt
**Beacon** là desktop app đa nền tảng (Electron, macOS & Windows) do Sơn tự thiết kế và xây một mình — "hệ điều hành AI cho kỹ sư phần mềm": theo dõi PR đa repo với AI tự review code (Claude), quản lý sprint Jira đa dự án, lịch trình thích ứng học tốc độ làm việc thật của người dùng, Knowledge Hub biến tri thức team thành agent. Kiến trúc: renderer 12 module viết JavaScript thuần (~13.9K dòng, không framework), preload bridge 60 kênh IPC được liệt kê tường minh (contextIsolation), main process 13 feature store, MongoDB offline-first với differential sync, chỉ 2 runtime dependency. Đã ship 278+ bản build nội bộ, dùng hằng ngày. Điểm đặc biệt: Sơn công khai cả điểm yếu (chưa có test suite tự động, credential DB nằm ở client kèm threat model và lộ trình sửa) — kỹ sư trưởng thành là biết gọi tên nợ kỹ thuật. Case study tương tác tại **/work/beacon**, có demo 8 module và link tải bản cài thật.

chunks.text:
Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
## Overview

A full-stack personal project built to deepen backend engineering — REST API design, authentication, and automated UI testing — applying software-engineering principles beyond testing. Source is on GitHub at github.com/sontbui/E-commerce-Platform.

## Does Son have backend experience?

Yes. This project is the clearest evidence: Son designed and built a **Spring Boot REST API** with **Spring Security and JWT** authentication over a **MySQL** relational store, with an **Angular** single-page front end (TypeScript · Material UI). It demonstrates real backend engineering — API design, auth, and data modelling — not just testing.

## 3-tier architecture

- **Angular SPA** (TypeScript · Material UI) — the client tier.
- **Spring Boot REST API** (Spring Security · JWT) — the application tier.
- **MySQL** — the relational data store.

## Testing

Selenium exercises the UI end-to-end, giving automated regression across the full stack — Son applying his testing discipline to his own product.

meta.title: Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
meta.summary: A personal full-stack project built to deepen backend engineering — REST API design, authentication, and automated UI testing — applying software-engineering principles beyond testing.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Test Infrastructure
## Overview

The platform behind the tests: Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one cross-platform execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux. This is a representative model of enterprise work, without proprietary detail.

## Context

Cybersecurity products aren't standard web apps — many are desktop applications that must be validated across three operating systems, which web-only tooling can't cover.

## Approach

Playwright for web and Squish for desktop/Qt GUIs feed a shared orchestration and test-data layer, so a heterogeneous suite reads as one signal to the team. Execution fans out across Windows, macOS, and Linux, then converges into unified reporting and flaky detection.

## Reliability

A flaky-test feedback loop — retry, quarantine, and trend analysis — took the suite from roughly 150 unstable tests to fewer than 20.

## System architecture

Runners (Playwright for web/API, Squish for desktop/Qt) → a shared Orchestration & Test-Data Layer → execution across the Windows / macOS / Linux matrix → Unified Reporting and Flaky Detection.

## CI/CD pipeline

Commit → Build → OS Matrix → Parallel Run → Aggregate → Flaky Gate. This is Son's cross-platform testing and CI/CD infrastructure experience: making a diverse, multi-OS suite behave like a single reliable signal.

meta.title: Enterprise Test Infrastructure
meta.summary: The cross-platform platform behind the tests — Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

chunks.text:
Technical Skills
## Automation & Testing

Son's strongest area. Playwright (advanced), Squish for desktop/Qt GUI testing (advanced), Selenium (proficient), API & contract testing (advanced), and test data management (proficient).

## AI & Platforms

Claude Agent SDK (proficient), Model Context Protocol / MCP (proficient), n8n workflow orchestration (proficient), and AI agent design (growing). These underpin his AI-powered test-automation platform.

## Languages & Backend

TypeScript (advanced), Java (proficient), Spring Boot (proficient), and MySQL (proficient). This backend experience is real and demonstrated in his full-stack e-commerce project, where he built a Spring Boot REST API with Spring Security and JWT over MySQL.

## DevOps & Tooling

CI/CD pipelines (advanced), Git automation (advanced), Docker (proficient), and Linux (proficient).

## Strongest engineering skills — summary

If asked for his strongest skills: test framework architecture in Playwright + TypeScript, cross-platform test infrastructure, CI/CD and Git automation, and applying AI agents (Claude Agent SDK + MCP) as engineering components. His backend skills (Java, Spring Boot, MySQL) are genuine but positioned as supporting his testing and platform work.

meta.title: Technical Skills
meta.summary: Son Bui's technical toolkit across automation & testing, AI & platforms, languages & backend, and DevOps & tooling.
